  • 2-6) Falling Squares (LC 699) — Segment Tree Max
  • 2-7) Maximum Sum Rectangle No Larger Than K (LC 363) — Prefix Sum + TreeSet
  • 2-8) Range Module (LC 715) — Segment Tree / TreeMap
  • 2-9) Longest Increasing Subsequence (LC 300) — Segment Tree on Values
  • 2-10) My Calendar II (LC 731) — Segment Tree / TreeMap

Segment Tree & Binary Indexed Tree (Fenwick Tree)

Overview

Segment Trees and Binary Indexed Trees (BIT/Fenwick Tree) are advanced data structures for efficiently handling range queries and updates on arrays.

Key Properties

  • Time Complexity: O(log n) for both query and update operations
  • Space Complexity: O(n) for BIT, O(4n) for Segment Tree
  • Core Idea: Precompute range information in tree structure for fast queries
  • When to Use: Range sum/min/max queries with updates, order statistics
  • Key Operations: Range query, point/range update, build tree

Core Characteristics

  • Range Queries: Sum, minimum, maximum, GCD, XOR over ranges
  • Point Updates: Modify single element efficiently
  • Range Updates: Modify entire ranges (with lazy propagation)
  • Space-Time Tradeoff: Extra space for faster query processing

Problem Categories

Category 1: Range Sum Queries

  • Description: Calculate sum over ranges with updates
  • Examples: LC 307 (Range Sum Query - Mutable), LC 308 (Range Sum Query 2D - Mutable)
  • Pattern: Use BIT or Segment Tree for point updates, range queries

Category 2: Range Minimum/Maximum Queries

  • Description: Find min/max in ranges with updates
  • Examples: LC 315 (Count of Smaller Numbers After Self), Custom RMQ problems
  • Pattern: Segment Tree with min/max operations

Category 3: Range Updates with Lazy Propagation

  • Description: Update entire ranges efficiently
  • Examples: Add value to range, set range to value
  • Pattern: Segment Tree with lazy propagation

Category 4: Order Statistics & Inversions

  • Description: Count smaller/larger elements, inversions
  • Examples: LC 315 (Count Smaller), LC 493 (Reverse Pairs), LC 327 (Count Range Sum)
  • Pattern: BIT with coordinate compression or merge sort

Data Structure Comparison

BIT vs Segment Tree Comparison

Aspect Binary Indexed Tree Segment Tree
Space O(n) O(4n)
Implementation Simple, short code More complex
Operations Sum, XOR, OR Any associative operation
Range Updates Difficult Easy with lazy propagation
1-indexed Natural fit Can be adapted
Query Types Prefix queries easy Arbitrary range queries

Templates & Algorithms

Template 1: Binary Indexed Tree (Fenwick Tree)

1class BIT:
2    """Binary Indexed Tree for range sum queries and point updates"""
3
4    def __init__(self, n):
5        self.n = n
6        self.tree = [0] * (n + 1)  # 1-indexed
7
8    def update(self, i, delta):
9        """Add delta to element at index i"""
10        while i <= self.n:
11            self.tree[i] += delta
12            i += i & (-i)  # Add lowest set bit
13
14    def query(self, i):
15        """Get prefix sum from 1 to i"""
16        total = 0
17        while i > 0:
18            total += self.tree[i]
19            i -= i & (-i)  # Remove lowest set bit
20        return total
21
22    def range_query(self, left, right):
23        """Get sum from left to right (inclusive)"""
24        if left > 1:
25            return self.query(right) - self.query(left - 1)
26        else:
27            return self.query(right)
28
29    def build(self, arr):
30        """Build BIT from array (1-indexed)"""
31        for i in range(1, len(arr)):
32            self.update(i, arr[i])

Template 2: Segment Tree (Range Sum)

1class SegmentTree:
2    """Segment Tree for range sum queries and point updates"""
3
4    def __init__(self, arr):
5        self.n = len(arr)
6        self.tree = [0] * (4 * self.n)  # 4x space for safety
7        self.build(arr, 1, 0, self.n - 1)
8
9    def build(self, arr, node, start, end):
10        """Build segment tree recursively"""
11        if start == end:
12            self.tree[node] = arr[start]
13        else:
14            mid = (start + end) // 2
15            self.build(arr, 2 * node, start, mid)
16            self.build(arr, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end)
17            self.tree[node] = self.tree[2 * node] + self.tree[2 * node + 1]
18
19    def update(self, node, start, end, idx, val):
20        """Update single element at index idx to val"""
21        if start == end:
22            self.tree[node] = val
23        else:
24            mid = (start + end) // 2
25            if idx <= mid:
26                self.update(2 * node, start, mid, idx, val)
27            else:
28                self.update(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, idx, val)
29            self.tree[node] = self.tree[2 * node] + self.tree[2 * node + 1]
30
31    def query(self, node, start, end, left, right):
32        """Query sum in range [left, right]"""
33        if right < start or end < left:
34            return 0  # No overlap
35
36        if left <= start and end <= right:
37            return self.tree[node]  # Complete overlap
38
39        # Partial overlap
40        mid = (start + end) // 2
41        left_sum = self.query(2 * node, start, mid, left, right)
42        right_sum = self.query(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, left, right)
43        return left_sum + right_sum
44
45    # Public interface methods
46    def point_update(self, idx, val):
47        """Update element at index idx to val"""
48        self.update(1, 0, self.n - 1, idx, val)
49
50    def range_sum(self, left, right):
51        """Get sum in range [left, right]"""
52        return self.query(1, 0, self.n - 1, left, right)

Template 3: Segment Tree with Lazy Propagation

1class LazySegmentTree:
2    """Segment Tree with lazy propagation for range updates"""
3
4    def __init__(self, arr):
5        self.n = len(arr)
6        self.tree = [0] * (4 * self.n)
7        self.lazy = [0] * (4 * self.n)
8        self.build(arr, 1, 0, self.n - 1)
9
10    def build(self, arr, node, start, end):
11        if start == end:
12            self.tree[node] = arr[start]
13        else:
14            mid = (start + end) // 2
15            self.build(arr, 2 * node, start, mid)
16            self.build(arr, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end)
17            self.tree[node] = self.tree[2 * node] + self.tree[2 * node + 1]
18
19    def push(self, node, start, end):
20        """Push lazy value down to children"""
21        if self.lazy[node] != 0:
22            self.tree[node] += self.lazy[node] * (end - start + 1)
23
24            if start != end:  # Not a leaf node
25                self.lazy[2 * node] += self.lazy[node]
26                self.lazy[2 * node + 1] += self.lazy[node]
27
28            self.lazy[node] = 0
29
30    def update_range(self, node, start, end, left, right, val):
31        """Add val to range [left, right]"""
32        self.push(node, start, end)
33
34        if start > right or end < left:
35            return
36
37        if start >= left and end <= right:
38            self.lazy[node] += val
39            self.push(node, start, end)
40            return
41
42        mid = (start + end) // 2
43        self.update_range(2 * node, start, mid, left, right, val)
44        self.update_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, left, right, val)
45
46        self.push(2 * node, start, mid)
47        self.push(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end)
48        self.tree[node] = self.tree[2 * node] + self.tree[2 * node + 1]
49
50    def query_range(self, node, start, end, left, right):
51        """Query sum in range [left, right]"""
52        if start > right or end < left:
53            return 0
54
55        self.push(node, start, end)
56
57        if start >= left and end <= right:
58            return self.tree[node]
59
60        mid = (start + end) // 2
61        left_sum = self.query_range(2 * node, start, mid, left, right)
62        right_sum = self.query_range(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, left, right)
63        return left_sum + right_sum
64
65    # Public interface
66    def range_add(self, left, right, val):
67        """Add val to range [left, right]"""
68        self.update_range(1, 0, self.n - 1, left, right, val)
69
70    def range_sum(self, left, right):
71        """Get sum in range [left, right]"""
72        return self.query_range(1, 0, self.n - 1, left, right)

Template 4: 2D Binary Indexed Tree

1class BIT2D:
2    """2D Binary Indexed Tree for 2D range sum queries"""
3
4    def __init__(self, rows, cols):
5        self.rows = rows
6        self.cols = cols
7        self.tree = [[0] * (cols + 1) for _ in range(rows + 1)]
8
9    def update(self, row, col, delta):
10        """Add delta to element at (row, col)"""
11        orig_col = col
12        while row <= self.rows:
13            col = orig_col
14            while col <= self.cols:
15                self.tree[row][col] += delta
16                col += col & (-col)
17            row += row & (-row)
18
19    def query(self, row, col):
20        """Get sum from (1,1) to (row, col)"""
21        total = 0
22        orig_col = col
23        while row > 0:
24            col = orig_col
25            while col > 0:
26                total += self.tree[row][col]
27                col -= col & (-col)
28            row -= row & (-row)
29        return total
30
31    def range_query(self, row1, col1, row2, col2):
32        """Get sum in rectangle from (row1, col1) to (row2, col2)"""
33        return (self.query(row2, col2) -
34                self.query(row1 - 1, col2) -
35                self.query(row2, col1 - 1) +
36                self.query(row1 - 1, col1 - 1))

LeetCode Problems & Solutions

Range Sum Query Problems

Problem LC # Data Structure Difficulty Key Technique
Range Sum Query - Immutable 303 Prefix Sum Easy Simple prefix array
Range Sum Query - Mutable 307 BIT/Segment Tree Medium Point update, range query
Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable 304 2D Prefix Sum Medium 2D prefix array
Range Sum Query 2D - Mutable 308 2D BIT Hard 2D point update, range query

Order Statistics Problems

Problem LC # Data Structure Difficulty Key Technique
Count of Smaller Numbers After Self 315 BIT + Compression Hard Coordinate compression
Reverse Pairs 493 BIT/Merge Sort Hard Count inversions
Count of Range Sum 327 BIT + Prefix Sum Hard Coordinate compression

LC 307: Range Sum Query - Mutable

1class NumArray:
2    """Range Sum Query with updates using BIT"""
3
4    def __init__(self, nums):
5        self.nums = [0] + nums  # Make 1-indexed
6        self.bit = BIT(len(nums))
7
8        # Build BIT
9        for i in range(1, len(self.nums)):
10            self.bit.update(i, self.nums[i])
11
12    def update(self, index, val):
13        """Update element at index to val"""
14        index += 1  # Convert to 1-indexed
15        delta = val - self.nums[index]
16        self.nums[index] = val
17        self.bit.update(index, delta)
18
19    def sumRange(self, left, right):
20        """Sum elements from left to right"""
21        return self.bit.range_query(left + 1, right + 1)
22
23# Alternative using Segment Tree
24class NumArraySegTree:
25    def __init__(self, nums):
26        self.seg_tree = SegmentTree(nums)
27        self.nums = nums
28
29    def update(self, index, val):
30        self.nums[index] = val
31        self.seg_tree.point_update(index, val)
32
33    def sumRange(self, left, right):
34        return self.seg_tree.range_sum(left, right)

LC 315: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self

1def countSmaller(nums):
2    """Count smaller numbers after self using BIT"""
3    if not nums:
4        return []
5
6    # Coordinate compression
7    sorted_nums = sorted(set(nums))
8    rank = {num: i + 1 for i, num in enumerate(sorted_nums)}
9
10    bit = BIT(len(sorted_nums))
11    result = []
12
13    # Process from right to left
14    for i in range(len(nums) - 1, -1, -1):
15        # Count numbers smaller than nums[i]
16        count = bit.query(rank[nums[i]] - 1) if rank[nums[i]] > 1 else 0
17        result.append(count)
18
19        # Add current number to BIT
20        bit.update(rank[nums[i]], 1)
21
22    return result[::-1]  # Reverse to get correct order
23
24# Alternative using merge sort
25def countSmallerMergeSort(nums):
26    """Using merge sort to count inversions"""
27    def mergeSort(arr):
28        if len(arr) <= 1:
29            return arr, [0] * len(arr)
30
31        mid = len(arr) // 2
32        left, left_counts = mergeSort(arr[:mid])
33        right, right_counts = mergeSort(arr[mid:])
34
35        merged = []
36        counts = [0] * len(arr)
37        i = j = 0
38
39        while i < len(left) and j < len(right):
40            if left[i][0] <= right[j][0]:
41                merged.append(left[i])
42                counts[left[i][1]] += j  # j elements from right are smaller
43                i += 1
44            else:
45                merged.append(right[j])
46                j += 1
47
48        while i < len(left):
49            merged.append(left[i])
50            counts[left[i][1]] += j
51            i += 1
52
53        while j < len(right):
54            merged.append(right[j])
55            j += 1
56
57        return merged, counts
58
59    # Create (value, original_index) pairs
60    indexed_nums = [(nums[i], i) for i in range(len(nums))]
61    _, counts = mergeSort(indexed_nums)
62    return counts

LC 493: Reverse Pairs

1def reversePairs(nums):
2    """Count reverse pairs using BIT and coordinate compression"""
3    if not nums:
4        return 0
5
6    # Get all possible values (including doubled values)
7    values = set(nums)
8    for num in nums:
9        values.add(2 * num)
10
11    # Coordinate compression
12    sorted_values = sorted(values)
13    rank = {val: i + 1 for i, val in enumerate(sorted_values)}
14
15    bit = BIT(len(sorted_values))
16    count = 0
17
18    for num in reversed(nums):
19        # Count how many numbers > 2 * num are already seen
20        target_rank = rank[2 * num]
21        # Query from target_rank+1 to end
22        if target_rank < len(sorted_values):
23            count += bit.query(len(sorted_values)) - bit.query(target_rank)
24
25        # Add current number to BIT
26        bit.update(rank[num], 1)
27
28    return count
29
30# Alternative merge sort approach
31def reversePairsMergeSort(nums):
32    def mergeSort(arr, start, end):
33        if start >= end:
34            return 0
35
36        mid = (start + end) // 2
37        count = mergeSort(arr, start, mid) + mergeSort(arr, mid + 1, end)
38
39        # Count reverse pairs
40        j = mid + 1
41        for i in range(start, mid + 1):
42            while j <= end and arr[i] > 2 * arr[j]:
43                j += 1
44            count += j - (mid + 1)
45
46        # Merge sorted arrays
47        arr[start:end + 1] = sorted(arr[start:end + 1])
48        return count
49
50    return mergeSort(nums, 0, len(nums) - 1)

LC 327: Count of Range Sum

1def countRangeSum(nums, lower, upper):
2    """Count range sums in [lower, upper] using BIT"""
3    if not nums:
4        return 0
5
6    # Compute prefix sums
7    prefix_sums = [0]
8    for num in nums:
9        prefix_sums.append(prefix_sums[-1] + num)
10
11    # Get all relevant values for coordinate compression
12    values = set(prefix_sums)
13    for ps in prefix_sums:
14        values.add(ps - lower)
15        values.add(ps - upper)
16
17    sorted_values = sorted(values)
18    rank = {val: i + 1 for i, val in enumerate(sorted_values)}
19
20    bit = BIT(len(sorted_values))
21    count = 0
22
23    for ps in prefix_sums:
24        # Count prefix sums in range [ps - upper, ps - lower]
25        left_rank = rank[ps - upper]
26        right_rank = rank[ps - lower]
27        count += bit.range_query(left_rank, right_rank)
28
29        # Add current prefix sum to BIT
30        bit.update(rank[ps], 1)
31
32    return count

Advanced Techniques

Coordinate Compression

1def coordinate_compress(arr):
2    """Compress coordinates for BIT usage"""
3    unique_vals = sorted(set(arr))
4    rank_map = {val: i + 1 for i, val in enumerate(unique_vals)}
5    return rank_map, len(unique_vals)
6
7def use_compression_example():
8    nums = [100, 1, 50, 200, 75]
9    rank_map, max_rank = coordinate_compress(nums)
10    # rank_map = {1: 1, 50: 2, 75: 3, 100: 4, 200: 5}
11
12    bit = BIT(max_rank)
13    for num in nums:
14        bit.update(rank_map[num], 1)  # Add frequency

Range Maximum Query (RMQ) Segment Tree

1class RMQSegmentTree:
2    """Segment Tree for Range Maximum Queries"""
3
4    def __init__(self, arr):
5        self.n = len(arr)
6        self.tree = [0] * (4 * self.n)
7        self.build(arr, 1, 0, self.n - 1)
8
9    def build(self, arr, node, start, end):
10        if start == end:
11            self.tree[node] = arr[start]
12        else:
13            mid = (start + end) // 2
14            self.build(arr, 2 * node, start, mid)
15            self.build(arr, 2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end)
16            self.tree[node] = max(self.tree[2 * node], self.tree[2 * node + 1])
17
18    def query_max(self, node, start, end, left, right):
19        if right < start or end < left:
20            return float('-inf')
21
22        if left <= start and end <= right:
23            return self.tree[node]
24
25        mid = (start + end) // 2
26        left_max = self.query_max(2 * node, start, mid, left, right)
27        right_max = self.query_max(2 * node + 1, mid + 1, end, left, right)
28        return max(left_max, right_max)
29
30    def range_max(self, left, right):
31        return self.query_max(1, 0, self.n - 1, left, right)

Performance Analysis

Time Complexity Comparison

Operation Naive Array BIT Segment Tree Sparse Table
Build O(1) O(n log n) O(n) O(n log n)
Point Update O(1) O(log n) O(log n) O(n)
Range Query O(n) O(log n) O(log n) O(1)
Range Update O(n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n)

Space Complexity

  • BIT: O(n) - very space efficient
  • Segment Tree: O(4n) - needs more space but more flexible
  • 2D BIT: O(n×m) - scales quadratically
  • Lazy Segment Tree: O(4n) - same as regular segment tree

Implementation Tips

Common Pitfalls & Solutions

1def bit_pitfalls():
2    """Common BIT implementation mistakes"""
3
4    # ❌ Wrong: 0-indexed BIT
5    # BIT naturally works with 1-indexed arrays
6
7    # ✅ Correct: Convert to 1-indexed
8    def update_correct(bit, index, delta):
9        index += 1  # Convert 0-indexed to 1-indexed
10        while index <= bit.n:
11            bit.tree[index] += delta
12            index += index & (-index)
13
14    # ❌ Wrong: Forgetting coordination compression
15    def wrong_approach(nums):
16        bit = BIT(max(nums))  # Might use too much memory
17
18    # ✅ Correct: Use coordinate compression
19    def correct_approach(nums):
20        rank_map, size = coordinate_compress(nums)
21        bit = BIT(size)
22        for num in nums:
23            bit.update(rank_map[num], 1)
24
25def segment_tree_tips():
26    """Segment Tree best practices"""
27
28    # Use 4n space allocation for safety
29    tree = [0] * (4 * n)
30
31    # Handle edge cases properly
32    def query(node, start, end, left, right):
33        if right < start or end < left:
34            return 0  # Return identity element
35        # ... rest of query logic

Summary & Quick Reference

When to Use Each Structure

Use Case Best Choice Why
Range Sum + Point Updates BIT Simple, space-efficient
Range Min/Max + Updates Segment Tree Supports any associative operation
Range Updates Lazy Segment Tree Efficient batch updates
2D Range Queries 2D BIT Natural extension
Count Inversions BIT + Compression Perfect for order statistics

Implementation Checklist

  • [ ] BIT: Remember 1-indexing, use coordinate compression for large values
  • [ ] Segment Tree: Allocate 4n space, handle query edge cases
  • [ ] Lazy Propagation: Implement push correctly, update children lazily
  • [ ] 2D Structures: Consider memory usage, test with small examples first

LeetCode Problem Categories

  • Range Sum: LC 303, 307, 308 (BIT/Segment Tree)
  • Order Statistics: LC 315, 327, 493 (BIT + Compression)
  • Dynamic Programming: Range DP with RMQ optimization
  • Geometry: 2D range queries, rectangle problems

LC Examples

2-1) Range Sum Query - Mutable (LC 307) — Segment Tree

Segment tree supports O(log N) range sum query and point update.

1// LC 307 - Range Sum Query - Mutable
2// IDEA: Segment Tree — build, update, query in O(log N)
3// time = O(log N) per op, space = O(N)
4class NumArray {
5    int[] tree;
6    int n;
7    public NumArray(int[] nums) {
8        n = nums.length;
9        tree = new int[2 * n];
10        // build leaves
11        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) tree[n + i] = nums[i];
12        // build internal nodes
13        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 1; i--) tree[i] = tree[2*i] + tree[2*i+1];
14    }
15    public void update(int i, int val) {
16        tree[n + i] = val;
17        for (int pos = (n + i) >> 1; pos >= 1; pos >>= 1)
18            tree[pos] = tree[2*pos] + tree[2*pos+1];
19    }
20    public int sumRange(int l, int r) {
21        int sum = 0;
22        for (l += n, r += n + 1; l < r; l >>= 1, r >>= 1) {
23            if ((l & 1) == 1) sum += tree[l++];
24            if ((r & 1) == 1) sum += tree[--r];
25        }
26        return sum;
27    }
28}

2-2) My Calendar I (LC 729) — Segment Tree / TreeMap for Interval Booking

Use TreeMap to check if new booking overlaps with any existing booking.

1// LC 729 - My Calendar I
2// IDEA: TreeMap — check overlap with floorKey and ceilingKey
3// time = O(log N) per booking, space = O(N)
4class MyCalendar {
5    TreeMap<Integer, Integer> calendar = new TreeMap<>();
6    public boolean book(int start, int end) {
7        Integer prev = calendar.floorKey(start);
8        Integer next = calendar.ceilingKey(start);
9        // No overlap if: prev booking ends before start, AND next booking starts after end
10        if ((prev == null || calendar.get(prev) <= start) &&
11            (next == null || next >= end)) {
12            calendar.put(start, end);
13            return true;
14        }
15        return false;
16    }
17}

This comprehensive guide covers the essential concepts and implementations for Segment Trees and Binary Indexed Trees, with practical examples from LeetCode problems.

2-3) Segment Tree Template — Range Sum with Lazy Propagation

Full segment tree with range-update and range-query in O(log N).

1// Segment Tree — Lazy Propagation Template
2// time = O(log N) per update/query, space = O(N)
3class SegTree {
4    int[] tree, lazy;
5    int n;
6    SegTree(int[] nums) {
7        n = nums.length;
8        tree = new int[4 * n];
9        lazy = new int[4 * n];
10        build(nums, 0, 0, n - 1);
11    }
12    private void build(int[] nums, int node, int l, int r) {
13        if (l == r) { tree[node] = nums[l]; return; }
14        int mid = (l + r) / 2;
15        build(nums, 2*node+1, l, mid);
16        build(nums, 2*node+2, mid+1, r);
17        tree[node] = tree[2*node+1] + tree[2*node+2];
18    }
19    private void pushDown(int node, int l, int r) {
20        if (lazy[node] != 0) {
21            int mid = (l + r) / 2;
22            tree[2*node+1] += (mid-l+1) * lazy[node]; lazy[2*node+1] += lazy[node];
23            tree[2*node+2] += (r-mid)   * lazy[node]; lazy[2*node+2] += lazy[node];
24            lazy[node] = 0;
25        }
26    }
27    void update(int node, int l, int r, int ql, int qr, int val) {
28        if (ql > r || qr < l) return;
29        if (ql <= l && r <= qr) { tree[node] += (r-l+1)*val; lazy[node] += val; return; }
30        pushDown(node, l, r);
31        int mid = (l + r) / 2;
32        update(2*node+1, l, mid, ql, qr, val);
33        update(2*node+2, mid+1, r, ql, qr, val);
34        tree[node] = tree[2*node+1] + tree[2*node+2];
35    }
36    int query(int node, int l, int r, int ql, int qr) {
37        if (ql > r || qr < l) return 0;
38        if (ql <= l && r <= qr) return tree[node];
39        pushDown(node, l, r);
40        int mid = (l + r) / 2;
41        return query(2*node+1, l, mid, ql, qr) + query(2*node+2, mid+1, r, ql, qr);
42    }
43}

2-4) Corporate Flight Bookings (LC 1109) — Difference Array

Range-add passengers [first, last]; prefix-sum to get totals per flight.

1// LC 1109 - Corporate Flight Bookings
2// IDEA: Difference array — range add O(1), prefix sum O(N) for result
3// time = O(N + Q), space = O(N)
4public int[] corpFlightBookings(int[][] bookings, int n) {
5    int[] diff = new int[n + 1];
6    for (int[] b : bookings) {
7        diff[b[0] - 1] += b[2];
8        if (b[1] < n) diff[b[1]] -= b[2];
9    }
10    for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) diff[i] += diff[i-1];
11    return Arrays.copyOf(diff, n);
12}

2-5) Count of Smaller Numbers After Self (LC 315) — Iterative Segment Tree

Build segment tree on value range; insert from right to left; query smaller prefix count.

1// LC 315 - Count of Smaller Numbers After Self (Segment Tree on values)
2// IDEA: Iterative seg tree on [0, 20001] value range; query prefix, then update
3// time = O(N log M), space = O(M)
4public List<Integer> countSmaller(int[] nums) {
5    int offset = 10001, size = 2 * offset + 1;
6    int[] tree = new int[2 * size];
7    Integer[] res = new Integer[nums.length];
8    for (int i = nums.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
9        int val = nums[i] + offset;
10        res[i] = queryTree(tree, size, 0, val - 1);
11        updateTree(tree, size, val);
12    }
13    return Arrays.asList(res);
14}
15private void updateTree(int[] t, int n, int i) { for (i+=n; i>0; i>>=1) t[i]++; }
16private int  queryTree(int[] t, int n, int l, int r) {
17    int s=0; for(l+=n,r+=n+1; l<r; l>>=1,r>>=1) {if((l&1)==1)s+=t[l++];if((r&1)==1)s+=t[--r];} return s;
18}

2-6) Falling Squares (LC 699) — Segment Tree Max

Each square lands on the highest existing height in its range; track running max height.

1// LC 699 - Falling Squares (naive O(N^2); segment tree gives O(N log N))
2// IDEA: For each square compute max height in its column range, then update
3// time = O(N^2), space = O(N)
4public List<Integer> fallingSquares(int[][] positions) {
5    List<Integer> ans = new ArrayList<>();
6    int[] heights = new int[positions.length];
7    int maxH = 0;
8    for (int i = 0; i < positions.length; i++) {
9        int l = positions[i][0], sz = positions[i][1], r = l + sz;
10        heights[i] = sz;
11        for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
12            int lj = positions[j][0], rj = lj + positions[j][1];
13            if (lj < r && l < rj)                    // overlap
14                heights[i] = Math.max(heights[i], heights[j] + sz);
15        }
16        maxH = Math.max(maxH, heights[i]);
17        ans.add(maxH);
18    }
19    return ans;
20}

2-7) Maximum Sum Rectangle No Larger Than K (LC 363) — Prefix Sum + TreeSet

Fix column bounds; compress to 1D row sums; use TreeSet to find max sum ≤ k.

1// LC 363 - Max Sum of Rectangle No Larger Than K
2// IDEA: Fix left/right cols; 1D Kadane + TreeSet for sum <= k constraint
3// time = O(M^2 * N log N), space = O(N)
4public int maxSumSubmatrix(int[][] matrix, int k) {
5    int m = matrix.length, n = matrix[0].length, ans = Integer.MIN_VALUE;
6    for (int l = 0; l < n; l++) {
7        int[] rowSum = new int[m];
8        for (int r = l; r < n; r++) {
9            for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) rowSum[i] += matrix[i][r];
10            TreeSet<Integer> set = new TreeSet<>();
11            set.add(0);
12            int curr = 0;
13            for (int s : rowSum) {
14                curr += s;
15                Integer ceiling = set.ceiling(curr - k);
16                if (ceiling != null) ans = Math.max(ans, curr - ceiling);
17                set.add(curr);
18            }
19        }
20    }
21    return ans;
22}

2-8) Range Module (LC 715) — Segment Tree / TreeMap

Track which ranges are tracked; add, remove, and query ranges efficiently.

1// LC 715 - Range Module (TreeMap approach)
2// IDEA: TreeMap<start, end> — merge on addRange, split on removeRange
3// time = O(N log N) per op, space = O(N)
4class RangeModule {
5    TreeMap<Integer, Integer> map = new TreeMap<>();
6    public void addRange(int left, int right) {
7        Integer lo = map.floorKey(left), hi = map.floorKey(right);
8        if (lo != null && map.get(lo) >= left) left = lo;
9        if (hi != null && map.get(hi) > right) right = map.get(hi);
10        map.subMap(left, right).clear();
11        map.put(left, right);
12    }
13    public boolean queryRange(int left, int right) {
14        Integer lo = map.floorKey(left);
15        return lo != null && map.get(lo) >= right;
16    }
17    public void removeRange(int left, int right) {
18        Integer lo = map.floorKey(left), hi = map.floorKey(right);
19        if (hi != null && map.get(hi) > right) map.put(right, map.get(hi));
20        if (lo != null && map.get(lo) > left)  map.put(lo, left);
21        map.subMap(left, right).clear();
22    }
23}

2-9) Longest Increasing Subsequence (LC 300) — Segment Tree on Values

Segment tree on compressed values; query max LIS length for values < current, then update.

1// LC 300 - LIS via Segment Tree (max query on value range)
2// IDEA: Compress values; seg tree max query for all smaller values; update at current
3// time = O(N log N), space = O(N)
4public int lengthOfLIS(int[] nums) {
5    int[] sorted = nums.clone();
6    Arrays.sort(sorted);
7    Map<Integer, Integer> rank = new HashMap<>();
8    int r = 1;
9    for (int v : sorted) if (!rank.containsKey(v)) rank.put(v, r++);
10    int n = r - 1;
11    int[] tree = new int[2 * (n + 1)];
12    int ans = 0;
13    for (int num : nums) {
14        int pos = rank.get(num);
15        int best = qmax(tree, n, 1, pos - 1) + 1;
16        ans = Math.max(ans, best);
17        umax(tree, n, pos, best);
18    }
19    return ans;
20}
21private int  qmax(int[] t, int n, int l, int r) { int res=0; for(l+=n,r+=n+1;l<r;l>>=1,r>>=1){if((l&1)==1)res=Math.max(res,t[l++]);if((r&1)==1)res=Math.max(res,t[--r]);}return res; }
22private void umax(int[] t, int n, int i, int v) { for(i+=n;i>0;i>>=1) t[i]=Math.max(t[i],v); }

2-10) My Calendar II (LC 731) — Segment Tree / TreeMap

Allow at most 2 overlapping bookings; reject if a third overlap would occur.

1// LC 731 - My Calendar II
2// IDEA: Two TreeMaps — bookings and double-bookings; reject if new interval hits double-booked region
3// time = O(N^2) worst, space = O(N)
4class MyCalendarTwo {
5    List<int[]> single = new ArrayList<>(), overlap = new ArrayList<>();
6    public boolean book(int start, int end) {
7        for (int[] o : overlap)
8            if (o[0] < end && start < o[1]) return false;
9        for (int[] s : single) {
10            int lo = Math.max(s[0], start), hi = Math.min(s[1], end);
11            if (lo < hi) overlap.add(new int[]{lo, hi});
12        }
13        single.add(new int[]{start, end});
14        return true;
15    }
16}
← Back to Cheat Sheets Edit on GitHub →
← Scanning LineSet →

CS_basics - Computer Science Fundamentals & Interview Preparation

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
